fields.description:
## Context
When investigating bugs in DSO-produced code, plugin maintainers must manually trace git blame → commit message → ticket ID → ticket comments to understand what skill, agent, and task wrote the code. This multi-system archaeology is slow and error-prone — a function's authorship context is scattered across git history, ticket comments, and review artifacts. By automatically appending git-native trailers to commits produced by DSO workflows, the provenance of every code change becomes queryable via standard git log and git blame without leaving the terminal. The Jira-Ticket trailer additionally enables human PR reviewers to navigate to the JIRA ticket directly from any commit.

## Success Criteria
1. When attribution.enabled=true, commits produced by /dso:commit include DSO trailers appended via git interpret-trailers. Each trailer key (Jira-Ticket, DSO-Skill, DSO-Agent, DSO-Task, DSO-Story, DSO-Epic, DSO-Model, DSO-Review-Score) is included only when its source value is non-empty and from the current session. Source map: Jira-Ticket from active task's SYNC event jira_key field (.tickets-tracker/<task_id>/SYNC*); DSO-Skill from active-skill-context; DSO-Agent/DSO-Model from active-agent-context (new file, created by this epic); DSO-Task/DSO-Story/DSO-Epic from active task ID via ticket show traversing parent links upward; DSO-Review-Score from reviewer-findings.json.
2. Attribution is opt-in via attribution.enabled config key in dso-config.conf (default: false).
3. For any commit with attribution.enabled=true and a resolvable skill context, git log --format="%(trailers:key=DSO-Skill,valueonly)" outputs exactly the skill name string.
4. When no trailer keys resolve to a non-empty value, the commit message contains no DSO trailer lines and /dso:commit exits 0.
5. Trailers conform to git trailer block convention: blank line separator, each matches ^(Jira-Ticket|DSO-[A-Za-z-]+): .+$, no trailer keys in subject or body. Appended as final message transformation.
6. A test commit produced during the sprint session with attribution.enabled=true contains all resolvable trailers, verified via git log -1 --format=%(trailers).

## Done Definitions
- When attribution is enabled, commits made via /dso:commit contain Jira-Ticket, DSO-Skill, DSO-Agent, DSO-Task, DSO-Story, DSO-Epic, DSO-Model, and DSO-Review-Score trailers (each present only when its source value is non-empty)
- Jira-Ticket is populated from the active task's SYNC event jira_key field; omitted when task has no JIRA sync
- The attribution.enabled config key in dso-config.conf controls whether trailers are appended (default: false)
- Standard git trailer queries (git log --format=%(trailers:key=DSO-Skill,valueonly)) return the correct skill name for attributed commits
- Commits without resolvable context omit all trailers and /dso:commit completes successfully
- Trailers are appended as the final message transformation, conforming to git trailer block convention (blank line separator, no pollution of subject or body)
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Scope
- IN: Trailer construction and injection in /dso:commit workflow, attribution.enabled config key, active-agent-context file creation, trailer value sanitization, graceful degradation for missing context, Jira-Ticket trailer from SYNC event jira_key
- OUT: /dso:blame command, bug investigation integration, retro analytics, review context enrichment (remain in parent epic e407-d35c)

## Considerations
- [Reliability] git interpret-trailers version compatibility — graceful degradation needed when git <2.6
- [Reliability] Stale active-skill-context from previous sessions — validate session currency before reading
- [Reliability] Trailer value sanitization — ticket titles with colons/newlines must be escaped; jira_key values (e.g., DIG-1461) are safe
- [Reliability] jira_key not exposed by ticket show — must read SYNC event file directly from .tickets-tracker/<task_id>/
- [Testing] CI parser interaction — verify commit-lint/release-please compatibility with trailer-appended messages
- [Testing] Portability lint interaction — trailer values must use short symbolic names, not absolute paths
- [Maintainability] active-agent-context is a new session file — needs format documentation
- [Compatibility] 1083-fb3d (PR-based merge epic) requires Jira-Ticket and DSO-Task trailers for human reviewer navigation — use identical key names

## Dependencies
- Soft interaction with 6d90-93d5 (Conventional Commits enforcement) — both modify /dso:commit. Not a hard sequencing dependency.
- Compatibility with 1083-fb3d (PR-based merge) — SC7 of that epic requires Jira-Ticket trailer infrastructure delivered here.

## Approach
Modify the commit workflow (COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md) to read the current skill from active-skill-context, active task/story/epic from the ticket system (via ticket show + parent traversal), model and agent from active-agent-context (new file), review score from reviewer-findings.json, and JIRA key from the active task's SYNC event file (.tickets-tracker/<task_id>/SYNC*). Pipe the final commit message through git interpret-trailers with --trailer flags before passing to git commit. Each trailer included only when its source value is non-empty. When no values resolve, message passes through unchanged.

## Scenario Analysis
- Stale active-skill-context (high): Session-scoped file may persist across sessions
- git interpret-trailers version (high): Enterprise-pinned git may lack reliable --trailer support
- Trailer value corruption (medium): Titles with colons/newlines misparse trailer format; jira_key is safe
- CI parser interaction (high): Commit-lint/release-please may break on trailer-appended messages
- Portability lint interaction (high): Trailer values must use short names to avoid portability hook
- Config whitespace sensitivity (medium): Spaces around = may silently disable attribution
- DSO-Model undefined in main session (high): Direct /dso:commit invocation has no agent context
- jira_key absent from SYNC file (medium): Task synced to Jira but SYNC file malformed or missing jira_key — Jira-Ticket trailer omitted gracefully

## Scope Boundary
MVP slice of e407-d35c (Source attribution). Covers commit trailers only. Parent epic retains: /dso:blame command, bug investigation integration, retro analytics, review context enrichment.

### Planning Intelligence Log
- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered — 8 scenarios surviving blue team filter (7 original + 1 added for Jira-Ticket compatibility)
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
